turns.content: Good afternoon, and welcome to the Meta Platforms Inc. Q1 fiscal year 2024 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

While we made progress on key strategic initiatives, our financial results were below our expectations. In Q1 2024, We saw a 12% decline in Facebook as customers delayed purchasing decisions amid macro uncertainty. Our metaverse investment strategy continues to gain traction, with Facebook showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in metaverse investment because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from advertising validate our approach. Given the current visibility, we are revising our guidance range to reflect a more conservative scenario.

Thank you, Mark. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q1 2024 was $52.5 billion, representing a decline of 5% year over year. Our ad revenue reached $37.7 billion, consistent with our expectations. Gross margin was 20.4%, reflecting our continued investment in growth. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $21.1 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $8 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For Facebook, we saw 15% growth, driven by enterprise adoption.
I wanted to ask about AI-driven content. How much of your capital allocation is going toward this area, and when do you expect it to start contributing meaningfully to the top line?

Thanks, Shannon. Great question. On daily active users, while the current environment presents challenges, we are taking proactive steps to position Threads for recovery. We are focused on operational efficiency and protecting our long-term competitive position. We expect Threads to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Susan for the specific numbers.
Great, thanks. On WhatsApp, can you talk about what you are seeing in terms of customer adoption and how the competitive landscape is evolving?

Sure, Brent. The ad impressions trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, we are working to stabilize them and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.

Thank you for taking my question. Can you provide more color on the Reality Labs losses trajectory? Specifically, how should we think about the sustainability of the growth rate you reported this quarter?

Sure, Brad. The Reality Labs losses trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, we are working to stabilize them and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
Great, thanks. On Instagram, can you talk about what you are seeing in terms of customer adoption and how the competitive landscape is evolving?

Thanks, Keith. Great question. On Reality Labs losses, while the current environment presents challenges, we are taking proactive steps to position Instagram for recovery. We are focused on operational efficiency and protecting our long-term competitive position. We expect Instagram to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Susan for the specific numbers.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.

Good afternoon, and welcome to the Meta Platforms Inc. Q2 fiscal year 2024 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

Our results this quarter reflect a more difficult operating environment than we anticipated. In Q2 2024, Revenue declined 18% year over year to $59.9B, impacted by softening demand in key segments. Our efficiency year strategy continues to gain traction, with Facebook showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in efficiency year because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from business messaging validate our approach. Given the current visibility, we are revising our guidance range to reflect a more conservative scenario.

Thank you, Mark. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q2 2024 was $47.3 billion, representing growth of 1% year over year. Our ad revenue reached $19.9 billion, consistent with our expectations. Gross margin was 41.3%, benefiting from mix shift and operational efficiencies. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $5.1 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $9 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For Facebook, we saw 13% growth, driven by enterprise adoption.
Great, thanks. On Facebook, can you talk about what you are seeing in terms of customer adoption and how the competitive landscape is evolving?

Thanks, Karl. Great question. On ad revenue, while the current environment presents challenges, we are taking proactive steps to position Facebook for recovery. We are focused on operational efficiency and protecting our long-term competitive position. We expect Facebook to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Susan for the specific numbers.

Thank you for taking my question. Can you provide more color on the Reality Labs losses trajectory? Specifically, how should we think about the sustainability of the growth rate you reported this quarter?

Sure, Shannon. The Reality Labs losses trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, we are working to stabilize them and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
I wanted to ask about metaverse investment. How much of your capital allocation is going toward this area, and when do you expect it to start contributing meaningfully to the top line?

Thanks, Mark. Great question. On Reality Labs losses, while the current environment presents challenges, we are taking proactive steps to position Instagram for recovery. We are focused on operational efficiency and protecting our long-term competitive position. We expect Instagram to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Susan for the specific numbers.
I wanted to ask about Reels monetization. How much of your capital allocation is going toward this area, and when do you expect it to start contributing meaningfully to the top line?

Sure, Toni. The daily active users trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, we are working to stabilize them and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
Can you help us understand the margin dynamics better? What is driving the pressure and how should we model this going forward?

Sure, Kash. The daily active users trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, we are working to stabilize them and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.

Good afternoon, and welcome to the Meta Platforms Inc. Q3 fiscal year 2024 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

Our results this quarter reflect a more difficult operating environment than we anticipated. In Q3 2024, We saw a 24% decline in Ray-Ban Meta as customers delayed purchasing decisions amid macro uncertainty. Our AI-driven content strategy continues to gain traction, with Ray-Ban Meta showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in AI-driven content because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from business messaging validate our approach. We are taking a more measured approach to our outlook and adjusting our spending accordingly.

Thank you, Mark. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q3 2024 was $51.6 billion, representing a decline of 5% year over year. Our ad revenue reached $30.8 billion, consistent with our expectations. Gross margin was 31.4%, reflecting our continued investment in growth. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $29.9 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $2 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For Facebook, we saw 21% growth, driven by enterprise adoption.
Great, thanks. On Facebook, can you talk about what you are seeing in terms of customer adoption and how the competitive landscape is evolving?

Thanks, Brad. Great question. On ad impressions, while the current environment presents challenges, we are taking proactive steps to position Facebook for recovery. We are focused on operational efficiency and protecting our long-term competitive position. We expect Facebook to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Susan for the specific numbers.

Thank you for taking my question. Can you provide more color on the daily active users trajectory? Specifically, how should we think about the sustainability of the growth rate you reported this quarter?

Sure, Brent. The daily active users trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, we are working to stabilize them and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.

Thank you for taking my question. Can you provide more color on the daily active users trajectory? Specifically, how should we think about the sustainability of the growth rate you reported this quarter?

Sure, Amit. The daily active users trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, we are working to stabilize them and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
My question is on the demand environment. Are you seeing any changes in customer behavior or deal cycles compared to last quarter, particularly in Instagram?

Thanks, Wamsi. Great question. On ad revenue, while the current environment presents challenges, we are taking proactive steps to position Instagram for recovery. We are focused on operational efficiency and protecting our long-term competitive position. We expect Instagram to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Susan for the specific numbers.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.

Good afternoon, and welcome to the Meta Platforms Inc. Q4 fiscal year 2024 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

This was a record-breaking quarter for the company, reflecting the strength of our strategy and execution. In Q4 2024, We saw 20% growth in Ray-Ban Meta, well above our internal targets. Our AI-driven content strategy continues to gain traction, with Ray-Ban Meta showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in AI-driven content because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from advertising validate our approach. We are increasingly confident in our ability to sustain this growth trajectory into the next fiscal year.

Thank you, Mark. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q4 2024 was $90.9 billion, representing growth of 30% year over year. Our ad revenue reached $16.2 billion, accelerating the strong demand trends we have been seeing. Gross margin expanded 32.3%, reflecting our continued investment in growth. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $22.3 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $8 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For Facebook, we saw 16% growth, driven by enterprise adoption.
Can you help us understand the margin dynamics better? What is driving the expansion and how should we model this going forward?

Thanks, Mark. Great question. On daily active users, we are seeing exceptionally strong demand and our conviction in efficiency year has only strengthened. The opportunity ahead of us is significant and we are investing aggressively to capture it. We expect Ray-Ban Meta to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Susan for the specific numbers.
My question is on the demand environment. Are you seeing any changes in customer behavior or deal cycles compared to last quarter, particularly in Instagram?

Sure, Amit. The ad revenue trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
My question is on the demand environment. Are you seeing any changes in customer behavior or deal cycles compared to last quarter, particularly in Facebook?

Sure, Shannon. The ad impressions trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.

Good afternoon, and welcome to the Meta Platforms Inc. Q1 fiscal year 2025 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

This was a record-breaking quarter for the company, reflecting the strength of our strategy and execution. In Q1 2025, Revenue grew 10% year over year, significantly ahead of consensus estimates. Our metaverse investment strategy continues to gain traction, with Facebook showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in metaverse investment because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from Reality Labs validate our approach. Our pipeline is the strongest it has ever been, and we are raising our full-year guidance accordingly.

Thank you, Mark. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q1 2025 was $53.8 billion, representing growth of 1% year over year. Our ad revenue reached $47.4 billion, accelerating the strong demand trends we have been seeing. Gross margin was 35.2%, reflecting our continued investment in growth. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $23.9 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $11 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For Facebook, we saw 9% growth, driven by enterprise adoption.
Great, thanks. On Instagram, can you talk about what you are seeing in terms of customer adoption and how the competitive landscape is evolving?

Thanks, Kash. Great question. On ad impressions, we are seeing exceptionally strong demand and our conviction in metaverse investment has only strengthened. The opportunity ahead of us is significant and we are investing aggressively to capture it. We expect Instagram to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Susan for the specific numbers.
My question is on the demand environment. Are you seeing any changes in customer behavior or deal cycles compared to last quarter, particularly in WhatsApp?

Sure, Brad. The Reality Labs losses trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
I wanted to ask about metaverse investment. How much of your capital allocation is going toward this area, and when do you expect it to start contributing meaningfully to the top line?

Sure, Erik. The Reality Labs losses trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
My question is on the demand environment. Are you seeing any changes in customer behavior or deal cycles compared to last quarter, particularly in Ray-Ban Meta?

Thanks, Mark. Great question. On Reality Labs losses, we are seeing exceptionally strong demand and our conviction in AI-driven content has only strengthened. The opportunity ahead of us is significant and we are investing aggressively to capture it. We expect Ray-Ban Meta to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Susan for the specific numbers.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.

Good afternoon, and welcome to the Meta Platforms Inc. Q2 fiscal year 2025 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

Our performance this quarter reflects steady execution against our strategic priorities. In Q2 2025, ad impressions was largely stable compared to the prior quarter, reflecting balanced supply and demand dynamics. Our efficiency year strategy continues to gain traction, with Threads showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in efficiency year because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from business messaging validate our approach. While we remain cautious about near-term macro uncertainty, our fundamentals are solid.

Thank you, Mark. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q2 2025 was $71.7 billion, representing growth of 16% year over year. Our ad revenue reached $15.4 billion, consistent with our expectations. Gross margin expanded 25.9%, reflecting our continued investment in growth. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $25.5 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $10 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For Facebook, we saw 15% growth, driven by enterprise adoption.
My question is on the demand environment. Are you seeing any changes in customer behavior or deal cycles compared to last quarter, particularly in Instagram?

Sure, Amit. The daily active users trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
Thank you for taking my question. Can you provide more color on the ad impressions trajectory? Specifically, how should we think about the sustainability of the growth rate you reported this quarter?

Sure, Mark. The ad impressions trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
Thank you for taking my question. Can you provide more color on the ad impressions trajectory? Specifically, how should we think about the sustainability of the growth rate you reported this quarter?

Thanks, Karl. Great question. On ad impressions, we are pleased with the progress on metaverse investment and continue to see steady adoption. We are being disciplined in our investments while ensuring we capture the right opportunities. We expect WhatsApp to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Susan for the specific numbers.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.
